chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented to our hospital due to upper gastrointestinal hemorrhage.
history: The patient had a history of esophageal carcinoma and was admitted due to upper gastrointestinal hemorrhage. He underwent emergency endoscopy, which revealed an active pulsatile bleeding. Endoscopic hemostasis was unsuccessful.
exam: Vital signs: BP 90/60 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a stable patient with signs of hypovolemic shock.
labs: Hemoglobin: 6.5 g/dL, ECG: normal, Blood tests: normal liver and renal function, Coagulation profile: normal.
correct_diagnosis: Upper gastrointestinal hemorrhage caused by a pseudoaneurysm of the left intercostal artery.
differentials: Upper gastrointestinal hemorrhage caused by a pseudoaneurysm of the left intercostal artery.
correct_ranking: Upper gastrointestinal hemorrhage caused by a pseudoaneurysm of the left intercostal artery.
